title: Mathieu Jussaume

{{Short description|French international rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=October 2024}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Mathieu Jussaume |fullname = |image = MathieuJussaume.jpg |image_size = 285 |position = {{rlp|CE}}, {{rlp|SR}} |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|df=yes|1999|05|17}} |birth_place = [[Toulouse]], Haute-Garonne,
Occitania, France |height = {{convert|1.82|m|ftin|abbr=on|order=flip}} |weight = {{convert|93|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}} |club1 = [[Toulouse Olympique]] |year1start = 2019 |year1end = present |appearances1 = 116 |tries1 = 48 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 192 |teamA = [[France national rugby league team|France]] |yearAstart = 2019 |yearAend = |appearancesA = 1 |triesA = 0 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 0 |new = yes |source = {{Cite web|url=http://www.itsrugby.co.uk/joueurs/mathieu-jussaume-42263.html|title=Mathieu JUSSAUME - Player statistics - It's rugby|website=www.itsrugby.co.uk}}{{Rugby league records|title=Mathieu Jussaume|id=49}} |updated = 9 October 2025 }} '''Mathieu Jussaume''' (born 17 May 1999) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== Jussaume discovered [[rugby league]] at the age of four with US Plaisance Rugby XIII in [[Plaisance-du-Touch]] and remained with them through the junior ranks before turning to [[rugby union]]. He was with [[Stade Toulousain]] from 2010 to 2015{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/mathieu-jussaume-au-stade-toulousain/|title=Mathieu JUSSAUME au Stade Toulousain|date=April 1, 2021}} and throughout his youth, Jussaume continued to play both league and union before finally committing to league with Toulouse Olympique.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/mathieu-jussaume-poursuit-au-to/|title=Mathieu JUSSAUME poursuit au TO|date=August 31, 2020}} He is a product of the Toulouse academy system. ==Club career== ===Toulouse Olympique Broncos=== Jussaume graduated from Toulouse's "Centre de Formation" and made his debut for the Toulouse reserve side on 3 December 2017 coming off the bench in the 20–16 win at home to [[FC Lézignan XIII|Lézignan]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.itsrugby.co.uk/player-zoom-42263-xiiifrance-2017-game.html|title=Mathieu JUSSAUME - Player statistics - Elite France XIII 2017 - Games played - It's rugby|website=www.itsrugby.co.uk}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.ladepeche.fr/article/2017/12/04/2697535-lezignan-a-ete-trop-indiscipline.html|title=Lézignan-Corbières. Lézignan a été trop indiscipliné|website=ladepeche.fr}} He played in 13 of Toulouse's 18 league games in the [[Elite One Championship 2017–2018|Elite 1 2017–2018 season]]. His only points of the campaign came from a conversion in the 42–28 defeat at [[Limoux Grizzlies|Limoux]] in May.{{Cite web|url=http://www.itsrugby.co.uk/game-139810.html|title=Rugby - Match details - Limoux / Toulouse Broncos - 27/05/2018 - teams - Elite France XIII|website=www.itsrugby.co.uk}} In 2018/19 Jussaume played 9 times for the Broncos between the start of the [[Elite One Championship 2018–2019|Elite 1 2018–2019 season]] in December and early March. He then joined up with the first team for the [[2019 RFL Championship|2019 Championship]] season. ===Toulouse Olympique=== Jussaume made his debut for the first team on 24 February 2019 in the 26–24 win at [[Swinton Lions]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.itsrugby.co.uk/game-157610.html|title=Rugby - Match details - Swinton Lions / Toulouse Olymp. XIII - 24/02/2019 - teams - Kingstone Press Championship|website=www.itsrugby.co.uk}} in the [[2019 RFL Championship|2019 Championship]]. He went on to play in 23 of Toulouse's league and play-off matches in 2019, scoring 11 tries. At the end of the season, Jussaume was rewarded with a contract extension for the 2020 season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/le-to-xiii-compte-sur-mathieu-jussaume/|title=Le TO XIII compte sur Mathieu JUSSAUME|website=www.ffr13.fr}} In 2020, Jussaume played in only one of Toulouse's five matches, coming off the bench in [[Doncaster]] against [[Sheffield Eagles]],{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3221984|title=Sheffield Eagles v Toulouse Olympique XIII - Rugby League|website=BBC Sport}} before the season was cancelled due to [[COVID-19]]. The previous week he had played for the reserves in Elite 1 at [[Limoux Grizzlies|Limoux]]. On 31 August 2020, Toulouse announced that Jussaume had signed a new contract until the end of the 2021 season. The [[2021 RFL Championship|2021 season]] was Jussaume's breakthrough year, playing in all 15 league and play-off games, scoring 14 tries. He scored two tries in the [[Million Pound Game]] in a man of the match performance which saw Toulouse promoted to Super League for 2022.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58824126|title=Toulouse win promotion to Super League|via=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 26 October 2021, Toulouse announced that Jussaume had signed a new contract that would keep him at the club until the end of the 2024 season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/mathieu-jussaume-jusquen-2024-avec-le-to-xiii/|title=Mathieu JUSSAUME jusqu’en 2024 avec le TO XIII|date=October 28, 2021}} Jussaume played twelve games for Toulouse Olympique in the 2022 Super League season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated back the RFL Championship.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-suffering-untold-damage-due-to-relegation-from-super-league/|title=Toulouse suffering "untold damage" due to relegation from Super League|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} On 15 October 2023, Jussaume played in Toulouse Olympique's upset loss in the [[Million Pound Game]] against the [[London Broncos]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} ==Club statistics== {|class=wikitable style="text-align: center;" ! Year !! Club !! Competition !! Appearances !! Tries !! Goals !! Drop goals !! Points |- | [[Elite One Championship 2017-2018|2017-18]] || {{leagueicon|Toulouse|16}} [[Toulouse Olympique Broncos]] || [[Elite One Championship 2017–2018|Elite One Championship]] || 13 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 2 |- | [[Elite One Championship 2018-2019|2018-19]] || {{leagueicon|Toulouse|16}} [[Toulouse Olympique Broncos]] || [[Elite One Championship 2018–2019|Elite One Championship]] || 9 || 1 || 0 || 0 || 4 |- | [[Elite One Championship 2019–2020|2019-20]] || {{leagueicon|Toulouse|16}} [[Toulouse Olympique Broncos]] || [[Elite One Championship 2019–2020|Elite One Championship]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || 0 || 0 |- | 2019 || {{leagueicon|Toulouse|16}} [[Toulouse Olympique]] || [[2019 RFL Championship|Championship]] || 23 || 11 || 0 || 0 || 44 |- | 2020 || {{leagueicon|Toulouse|16}} [[Toulouse Olympique]] || [[2020 RFL Championship|Championship]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || 0 || 0 |- | 2021 || {{leagueicon|Toulouse|16}} [[Toulouse Olympique]] || [[2021 RFL Championship|Championship]] || 15 || 14 || 0 || 0 || 56 |- !colspan=3|Club career total | 62 || 26 || 1 || 0 || 106 |} ==International career== ===France U19s=== Jussaume was a member of the France squad that competed in the 2018 U19s European Championship and played in the final, which saw France beat England 26–24 to be crowned European Champions.{{Cite web|url=https://rugbyleagueu19euro.com/2018/08/12/m12-france-vs-england-26-24/|title=M12 France vs England 26-24 – Rugbyleagueu19euro.com|first=Office RL U19|last=EC}} ===France 9s=== The French squad that took part in the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]] in [[Sydney]], [[Australia]] in October 2019 included Jussaume. He played in the 23–6 victory over Wales, but didn't play in either of the two defeats to Lebanon or England.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/les-bleus-dominent-les-gallois-mais-chutent-devant-les-anglais/|title=Les Bleus dominent les Gallois mais chutent devant les Anglais|date=October 19, 2019}} ===France=== Jussaume was part of the expanded French squad after the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]] that played two games Down Under. He made his debut for the senior side on 25 October 2019 scoring a try in the 62–4 defeat to the Junior Kangaroos after coming on from the bench.{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2019/10/25/ponga-stars-in-junior-roos-romp/|title=Ponga stars in Junior Roos romp|date=October 25, 2019|website=Newcastle Knights}} He missed out on the final match of the tour against the Western Rams.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/la-solidarite-de-la-france-a-fini-par-payer/|title=La solidarité de la France a fini par payer|date=October 30, 2019}} On 23 October 2021 Jussaume earned his second cap in Perpignan in the 10-30 test match defeat to England.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3744700|title=France v England - Rugby League|website=BBC Sport}} ==International statistics== {|class=wikitable style="text-align: center;" !
